rules.name: Subpoena Authority
rules.text: The arbitrator may issue subpoenas for witness testimony and document production under FAA §7.

elements.name: Arbitrator Issuance
elements.text: Only the arbitrator may issue subpoenas under FAA §7.

elements.name: Written Testimony
elements.text: The arbitrator may require written testimony or documents prior to the close of the written hearing.

exceptions.name: beyond_reasonable_doubt
exceptions.text: Criminal burdens of proof do not apply to FAA subpoenas.

rules.name: Prima Facie Burden
rules.text: Subpoenas require a prima facie showing of relevance and materiality.

elements.name: Minimal Showing
elements.text: The requesting party must show minimal evidence that the subpoenaed material is relevant to the dispute.

elements.name: Materiality
elements.text: The subpoenaed material must have a logical connection to a claim or defense.

rules.name: NYBAR-Style Subpoena Structure
rules.text: Subpoenas must follow the structural conventions used in New York practice, adapted for FAA arbitration.

elements.name: Caption
elements.text: Subpoenas must include the forum name, case number, and party names.

elements.name: Description of Materials
elements.text: Requested documents must be described with reasonable particularity.

elements.name: Return Instructions
elements.text: Subpoenas must specify the method and deadline for returning documents or testimony.

rules.name: NYBAR-Style Service Requirements
rules.text: Subpoenas must be served in accordance with NYBAR-style service conventions adapted for FAA arbitration.

elements.name: Personal Service Preferred
elements.text: Subpoenas should be personally served on the witness or custodian of records when feasible.

elements.name: Alternative Service
elements.text: If personal service is impracticable, alternative service methods may be used consistent with jurisdictional rules.

elements.name: Proof of Service
elements.text: The serving party must provide a written declaration of service.

rules.name: NYBAR-Style Compliance Rules
rules.text: Witnesses must comply with subpoenas unless they demonstrate undue burden or privilege.

elements.name: Undue Burden
elements.text: A witness may object if compliance imposes an undue burden.

elements.name: Privilege
elements.text: Privileged materials are exempt from production.

elements.name: Motion to Quash
elements.text: Witnesses may request that the arbitrator quash or modify the subpoena.

definitions.text: A written order issued by the arbitrator requiring testimony or document production.
The individual responsible for maintaining the requested documents.
A level of hardship that justifies limiting or quashing a subpoena.
